          AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5   1
 2   S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY
 Introduction
01.


  AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5   3
                                   It is with great pride and genuine
                                  admiration that I acknowledge
                                  the remarkable efforts of Surf Life
                                  Saving Sydney (SLSS) over the past
                                  year. As Patron, I am constantly
                                  reminded of the heart, courage
                                  and selflessness that define this
                                  incredible organisation.
                                  In the last twelve months alone, our
                                  dedicated lifesavers have patrolled our
                                  beaches for more than 188,000 hours. In
                                  that time, they performed 913 rescues, took
                                  more than 51,000 preventative actions,
                                  and safeguarded an estimated 1.7 million
                                  beachgoers. These figures speak volumes,
                                  but even more meaningful is what they
                                  represent. Lives protected, tragedies
                                  averted, and communities served with
                                  unwavering commitment. The increasing
                                  focus on proactive prevention has not only
                                  reduced the number of rescues needed but
                                  has also underscored the value of foresight,
                                  vigilance, and education on our shores.
                                  Youth engagement continues to be a
                                  shining priority. Through dynamic Camps
                                  and Clinics that combine learning with
                                  leadership, fun with challenge, SLSS is
                                  nurturing the next generation of lifesavers
                                  during those crucial years where retention



    A MESSAGE
                                  can be a challenge. These young members
                                  are not just the future, they are already
                                  making a difference.It’s particularly inspiring



    FROM OUR
                                  to see membership grow beyond 20,000
                                  at a time when volunteering presents new
                                  challenges. The increasing representation
                                  of women, particularly in leadership roles
                                  is creating a more diverse, balanced, and
                                  forward-thinking movement.
Patron


                                  Congratulations to all members who were
                                  honoured nationally with the Order of
                                  Australia Medal and Emergency Services
                                  Medal. Your contributions uplift not only
                                  SLSS, but the broader community.
                                  I offer heartfelt congratulations to President
                                  Elissa Hancock and her dedicated Board
                                  of Directors. Your vision, energy, and
                                  devotion to innovation continue to guide
                                  this organisation with strength and purpose.
                                  Thank you for nurturing an environment
                                  where every member feels seen, supported
                                  and inspired.
                                  Finally, to each and every volunteer who
                                  gives their time, energy, and heart to this
                                  cause, thank you. Your actions save lives.
                                  Your example brings hope. And your
                                  presence on our beaches brings peace
                                  of mind to thousands. I wish you a safe,
                                  successful, and fulfilling summer season
                                  ahead.
                                  With deepest gratitude,


                                  Peter Pearce OAM
                                  Patron




4   S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY
 AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5   5
 President

                                   The 2024/25 Season brought out the very best
                                   in our Members. Across our beaches, clubs, and
                                   communities, we’ve witnessed exceptional service
                                   that has safeguarded countless lives, inspiring
                                   innovation that has strengthened our operations,
                                   and the unwavering dedication of our volunteers
                                   who give so freely of their time, skills, and energy.
                                   From the Patrolling Members on the sand to
                                   those working tirelessly behind the scenes, every
                                   individual has played a vital role in delivering the
                                   high standards of safety, education, and community
                                   connection that define Surf Life Saving Sydney.
                                   Time and time again, we’re reminded that Surf Life Saving is so
                                   much more than just an organisation. It’s a community, one built
                                   on care, shared purpose, and the belief that looking after others
                                   truly matters. Whether it’s on the beach, in the surf, in education
                                   courses or at youth events, the spirit of this movement continues
                                   to shine. Our reach has grown, but more importantly, our
                                   connections have deepened.
                                   This season, our Branch proudly surpassed 20,000 members, a
                                   powerful reflection of the trust our communities place in what
                                   we do. Together, our teams delivered more than 188,000 patrol
                                   hours, supported over 1.7 million beachgoers, completed 913
                                   rescues, and carried out more than 51,000 preventative actions.
                                   Each number tells a real story of lives safeguarded, challenges
                                   met, and communities made stronger.
                                   We’re particularly proud of the shift we continue to see from
                                   reactive to proactive lifesaving. Prevention has become
                                   a cornerstone of our patrol efforts, and it’s making a real
                                   difference. Thanks to early interventions and member vigilance,
                                   fewer people have needed rescuing. It’s a powerful testament to
                                   the calm, capable presence of our lifesavers and the strength of
                                   our training programs.
                                   None of this would be possible without the incredible people
                                   behind it all. Our volunteers remain the very heart of this
                                   organisation. Whether you’re on the sand, mentoring a young
                                   lifesaver, helping behind the scenes, or leading from the front,



     REPORT
                                   you matter. That’s why we are putting real effort into easing
                                   the burden on our clubs and improving support for members
                                   through better communication, leadership training, and access
                                   to mental health resources.



 6   S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY

the future of Surf Life Saving and it’s in good hands.

8   S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY
 Donations and
Sponsorship
Surf Life Saving Sydney is proud to acknowledge our sponsor’s contribution, both financially and
materially. Without sponsorship, ‘corporate, government and individual’, our task of providing viable
lifesaving services to the community of Sydney would be impossible. We are proud that here in our
Annual Report, the major public document of SLS Sydney, that we recognize and extend our sincere
thanks to all our sponsors and donors for their support. Support that is given without restriction or
interference with our objectives and achievements.

FUNDRAISING DISTRIBUTIONS                                           GOVERNMENT RECOGNTION

SLSS SPONSORS




SLSNSW SPONSORS




SLSA SPONSORS




                                                                                    AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5   9
                                    Surf Life Saving Sydney Branch supports the
                                   Eastern and Southern Sydney coastline from
                                   Sydney Harbour down to the Royal National Park,
                                   with its 15 Surf Life Saving Clubs and Rescue
                                   Services. Over the 2024/25 Season, Sydney Branch
                                   had over 20k members, with, across our beaches,
                                   188k patrolling hours, 1.7m estimated beachgoers,
                                   913 individual rescues, and 51k preventions. Whilst
                                   these numbers are an impressive representation
                                   of the work that our members undertake, they
                                   demonstrate an increase of hours from last year of
                                   hours, with preventions increasing at a rate higher
                                   than the increase of our members. Thankfully more
                                   preventions means fewer rescues.
                                   The work that our volunteers are undertaking is continuing to
                                   increase year on year and our focus as a branch is to provide
                                   support to assist them in delivering these services. It is
                                   imperative that we do everything that we can to minimise the
                                   overheads and logistics within our clubs, and to upskill, develop
President
                                   and promote our members in the delivery of their activities.
                                   Only by constantly reviewing the branch’s activities and events
                                   can we ensure that they remain relevant, timely, affordable and
                                   current, supporting our members as best they can.
                                   As a result, this season Sydney Branch has undertaken
                                   complete reviews across all our deliverables to identify what
                                   we deliver, and how it actually promotes the work that our
                                   amazing volunteers provide. From our outputs such as Youth
                                   Development, Member Services, Education, Lifesaving, Rescue
Deputy

                                   Services and Surf Sports, as well as our internal structures
                                   around IT, Finance and Strategy, we investigated and updated
                                   what, when, how and where we undertake our tasks to provide
                                   critical focus on our members and creating a better Sydney surf
                                   lifesaving community
                                   Our Portfolios have focused on identifying improvements
                                   such as,
                                   +   In Rescue Services, we onboarded Surf Rescue 30 ensuring
                                       proper governance practices were applied. Updated
                                       governance frameworks across all Rescue Services reducing
                                       duplication, knocking down internal silos and creating
                                       greater pathways for our volunteers. We’ve now ensured our
                                       volunteers can seamlessly progress between services.
                                   +   In Lifesaving we placed greater focus on the LSAs before
                                       implementation to reduce the burden on clubs during the
                                       review process. By ensuring clubs were informed they were
                                       able to engage with agency, delivering signed agreements
                                       far earlier than previously supplied
                                   +   In Youth and Juniors we increased our Camps and Clinics,
                                       ensuring that our enthusiastic young members are provided
                                       with educational programs, social engagement, physical
                                       challenge, team building and development opportunities,
                                       enabling ongoing participation at a time when our young
                                       members are departing.
                                   +   Our Members Services team redesigned their structure
                                       to ensure that focus aligned with membership needs. We
                                       hosted a Leadership & Development event for emerging
                                       talent, with guest speakers and networking opportunities,
                                       and a Mental Health First Aid Course with strong club
                                       representation keen for additional resources to support
                                       their vulnerable members




     REPORT
                                   +   Our Education team identified a backlog in processing our
                                       members and focused on identifying and onboarding new
                                       TAFs which enabled the backlog to be cleared.
